steps.title: Group the perfect square
steps.content:
The last three terms form the negative of a square.
$$x^2-c^2-4cd-4d^2=x^2-(c^2+4cd+4d^2)$$
$$c^2+4cd+4d^2=(c+2d)^2$$

steps.title: Substitute and factor
steps.content:
Since $a=c+2d$, the expression becomes a difference of squares.
$$x^2-(c+2d)^2=x^2-a^2$$
$$x^2-a^2=(x+a)(x-a)$$
The answer is C.
